reasoning: Tnfaip3 encodes the ubiquitin‑editing enzyme A20, a key negative regulator of NF‑κB and other inflammatory signaling pathways. CRISPRi‑mediated knockdown of Tnfaip3 removes this brake, leading to heightened NF‑κB activity and increased expression of many cytokines and stress‑responsive genes. Sec11c, however, encodes a subunit of the endoplasmic‑reticulum signal peptidase complex that is primarily involved in co‑translational protein processing. There is no established link between A20‑mediated NF‑κB signaling and transcriptional control of Sec11c, nor is Sec11c known to be an NF‑κB target gene. Consequently, loss of Tnfaip3 is unlikely to cause a consistent up‑ or down‑regulation of Sec11c expression.
